cases.passage: A simple pendulum is hung from the ceiling of a laboratory room. The length of the pendulum is 1 meter. When the pendulum is displaced by an angle of 10 degrees and released, it starts oscillating. The time period of the pendulum is measured to be 2 seconds. If the length of the pendulum is doubled, what will be the new time period? A simple pendulum is a classic example of a physical system that exhibits simple harmonic motion. The time period of a simple pendulum depends only on the length of the pendulum and the acceleration due to gravity. In this experiment, the length of the pendulum is doubled and we need to find the new time period.

questions.q: What is the time period of the given simple pendulum?
questions.explanation: The time period of the pendulum is given as 2 seconds.

questions.q: How does the time period of a simple pendulum depend on the length of the pendulum?
opts: It depends only on the length of the pendulum.
It depends only on the acceleration due to gravity.
It depends on both the length of the pendulum and the acceleration due to gravity.
It does not depend on the length of the pendulum.
questions.explanation: The time period of a simple pendulum depends on both the length of the pendulum and the acceleration due to gravity.

questions.q: What happens to the time period of the pendulum when its length is doubled?
opts: The time period remains the same.
The time period increases by a factor of 2.
questions.explanation: The time period of a simple pendulum is proportional to the square root of its length, so when the length is doubled, the time period increases by a factor of sqrt(2).

questions.q: Which of the following statements is true about the time period of a simple pendulum?
opts: It depends only on the length of the pendulum.
It depends only on the acceleration due to gravity.
It depends on both the length of the pendulum and the acceleration due to gravity.
It does not depend on the length of the pendulum.
questions.explanation: The time period of a simple pendulum depends on both the length of the pendulum and the acceleration due to gravity.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the time period of a simple pendulum and the length of the pendulum?
questions.explanation: The time period of a simple pendulum is proportional to the square root of its length, so T ∝ √L.

cases.passage: A mass-spring system is used to demonstrate the concept of simple harmonic motion. A block of mass 2 kg is attached to a spring with a spring constant of 100 N/m. The block is displaced by a distance of 0.1 m and then released. The resulting motion is observed to be simple harmonic motion. The time period of the motion is measured to be 0.5 seconds. If the spring constant is increased to 200 N/m, what will be the new time period of the motion?

questions.q: What is the time period of the given mass-spring system?
questions.explanation: The time period of the block is given as 0.5 seconds.

questions.q: How does the time period of a mass-spring system depend on the spring constant?
opts: It depends only on the spring constant.
It depends only on the mass of the block.
It depends on both the spring constant and the mass of the block.
It does not depend on the spring constant.
questions.explanation: The time period of a mass-spring system depends only on the mass of the block, not on the spring constant.

questions.q: What happens to the time period of the mass-spring system when the spring constant is increased?
opts: The time period remains the same.
questions.explanation: The time period of a mass-spring system depends only on the mass of the block, not on the spring constant, so the time period remains the same when the spring constant is increased.

questions.q: Which of the following statements is true about the time period of a mass-spring system?
opts: It depends only on the spring constant.
It depends only on the mass of the block.
It depends on both the spring constant and the mass of the block.
It does not depend on the spring constant.
questions.explanation: The time period of a mass-spring system depends only on the mass of the block, not on the spring constant.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the time period of a mass-spring system and the spring constant?
questions.explanation: The time period of a mass-spring system is proportional to the square root of the mass of the block, so T ∝ √m.
